title: calceolaria commutata
intro: Calceolaria commutata, also known as the pocket book or fairy bells, is a flowering plant that belongs to the Calceolaria genus. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and demulcent properties. The herbal preparations of Calceolaria commutata typically include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically proven.
